---
title: "drill 1"
author: "Sydney Thompson"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Chat Drill 1

## What we have to do

ChatGPT has given us a tibble to work with. Meanwhile, we need to fix this script we generated.

```{r}
library(tibble)
library(tidyverse)

df <- tibble(
  id = c(1,1,1,2,2,2,3,3,3,4,4,4),
  day = c("1","2","3","1","2","3","1","2","3","1","2","3"),
  group = c("A","A","A","B","B","B","A","A","A","B","B","B"),
  mood = c(5, 4, -999, 3, 2, 3, 4, 5, 5, 1, -999, 2),
  word_count = c("100","120","","80","90","85","110","","130","70","60","")
)
df
```

## The script

Imagine a grad student comes up to you and gives you a script. They say, “It works… I think.”

```{r}
df$day <- as.numeric(df$day) # redundant and don’t need

df2 <- df[df$group == "A" | df$group == "B", ] # redundant and don’t need

df2$mood2 <- ifelse(df2$mood == -999, NA, df2$mood) # can use mutate for this and na_if

means <- tapply(df2$mood2, df2$id, mean) # can use dplyr for this

df3 <- data.frame(id = names(means), avg = means) # get rid of, makes data confusing. puts the variable names as values for some reason??
df3
df3$id <- as.numeric(df3$id) # don’t need, leads to NA values
df3$id
df4 <- merge(df3, df2, by = "id") # also leads to NA values

df4 <- df4[!duplicated(df4$id), ] # gives nothing

df4$high_mood <- ifelse(df4$avg > 4, 1, 0) # gives a logical(0)

tapply(df4$high_mood, df4$group, mean) # gives a logical(0)

df4$word_count <- as.character(df4$word_count) # gives character(0)

df4$word_count[df4$word_count == ""] <- NA # why put an NA?? need to put an if statement here if you want the blank ones as NA

df4$word_count <- as.numeric(df4$word_count) # gives nothing

cor(df4$avg, df4$word_count) # gives an error
```

### Rewritten script

```{r}
# 1. clean data first
df <- df %>%
 mutate(
   mood = na_if(mood, -999),
   word_count = na_if(word_count, ""),
   word_count = as.numeric(word_count),
   day = as.numeric(word_count)
 )
# 2. find means
df <- df %>%
  group_by(id, group) %>% # keep groups attached to person
  summarize(
    mood_m = round(mean(mood, na.rm=TRUE), 2),
    wc_m = mean(word_count, na.rm=TRUE),
    .groups = "drop" # explicitly ungroups the result
  )
# 3. example analysis
cor(df$mood_m, df$wc_m, use = "complete.obs")
```
